prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f823c43c`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 task package for `f823c43c` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f823c43c/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f823c43c/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f823c43c/generator.py#L1). The verifier treats color `6` as corruption, finds the smallest row and column periods consistent with the non-`6` cells, recovers the clean motif, and retessellates it across the grid. The summary hint was used, not discarded; it matched the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. No shared primitive was added.

The generator stays close to the task family by sampling small `2x2` or `3x3` two-color wallpaper motifs like the official examples, tiling them over medium grids, protecting one aligned clean tile so the true period remains recoverable, and then scattering `6` noise elsewhere. Official verification passed `3/3`, the shared builder produced `1000` examples, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/f823c43c/previews/preview_01.png), which looks on-family.
